title: sideritis scordioides subsp. hirtula
intro: Sideritis scordioides subsp. hirtula is a subspecies of perennial plant within the genus Sideritis, commonly known as mountain tea or ironwort. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Sideritis scordioides subsp. hirtula include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory problems, as well as exhibiting potential neuroprotective and cardiovascular benefits. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed fresh or dried for later use.
